7-hour sleep; early riser will wake at 6am tomorrow; overnight beat spans correctly to next calendar date.

diary:
Woke up early and moved through my stretches like I always do—there's something grounding about meeting the day before it gets loud. The Woodland Park trails were perfect this morning, all green and quiet. Grabbed a smoothie bowl at Molly Moon's and felt properly fueled.

Teaching three classes back-to-back at CorePower left me pleasantly tired. The vinyasa flow had a good energy, and I managed to sneak in some personal practice between classes. Noticed the light rail closure had people rerouted, but my commute held steady. Grabbed lunch at Serious Pie—their grain bowls hit different on Mondays.

The yin class this afternoon felt especially needed; everyone seemed to sink deeper into the stretches. By the time I got home, I was ready for quiet. Read on the couch, journaled a bit, took an evening walk through Fremont. There's a contentment in these slower hours that I'm learning to protect.
thought_process: I stayed pretty grounded today despite the busyness. Teaching three classes meant I had to manage my energy carefully—I deliberately kept my personal practice in the morning and afternoon lighter so I could show up fully for students. The World Cup match didn't really pull at me; I'm not huge into soccer, and I knew my day was already structured. The light rail closure could've thrown me, but living and working where I do, it didn't impact my commute much. I think what shaped my day most was just the rhythm of it—moving from solitude to teaching to solitude again. By evening, I was genuinely tired in that good way, not depleted.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
